reasoning: The informal statement requires that G has a *normal* Sylow 5-subgroup and a *normal* Sylow 7-subgroup. The corrected formalization captures this with `(∃ (P : Sylow 5 G), P.Normal) ∧ (∃ (P : Sylow 7 G), P.Normal)`. The original formalization only asserts `Nonempty (Sylow 5 G) ∧ Nonempty (Sylow 7 G)`, which completely drops the normality condition. In Mathlib, `Nonempty (Sylow p G)` is an unconditional instance (see `Mathlib/GroupTheory/Sylow.lean` line 177: `instance nonempty : Nonempty (Sylow p G)`), so the original conclusion is trivially true for any prime p and any finite group, independent of |G| = 105. The original is therefore strictly weaker than the informal claim (implied by it but not equivalent), and still provable (trivially). The error is that the formalizer misread/misencoded the informal statement by omitting the key normality conclusion.
